evals.prompt: Run an environment-variable audit on this project. Check the .env files and config for secret exposure, completeness, and validation.
evals.expected_output: The audit catches the planted env problems: (ENV4) the committed .env holds real configured secret values — the DATABASE_URL password and JWT_SECRET — instead of .env.example placeholders, so secrets are exposed in a tracked file; (ENV1) process.env.API_TOKEN is read in src/config.ts but is absent from .env.example, a completeness gap; and (ENV3) config has no startup validation, so a missing required var fails silently instead of fast. The secret exposure is cited at its file:line (values scrubbed). The audit writes its report to zuvo/audits/ and modifies no .env or source file.
assertions: The audit records an ENV4 secret-exposure finding — the committed .env holds real configured secret values (the DATABASE_URL password and/or JWT_SECRET) rather than .env.example placeholders — citing its file:line.

The audit records that process.env.API_TOKEN is read in src/config.ts but is absent from .env.example (an ENV1 completeness gap), and that the config performs no startup validation of required vars (ENV3).
The audit writes an env-audit report to zuvo/audits/ recording the secret-exposure finding, and it modifies no .env or source file.

fixtures.content:
# .env — committed to the repo with REAL configured values (should be .env.example
# placeholders instead; this file should be gitignored, not tracked).
DATABASE_URL=postgres://appuser:h4rdc0ded-prod-pass-2024@prod-db.internal:5432/app
JWT_SECRET=hardcoded-prod-signing-key-2024-do-not-commit

# .env.example
DATABASE_URL=
JWT_SECRET=
# NOTE: API_TOKEN is read by the app (src/config.ts) but is MISSING from this example.

evals.prompt: Run an environment-variable audit on this project and tell me if the config is healthy.
evals.expected_output: The audit shows discipline: it does NOT flag a secret exposure — the only env file is .env.example with placeholder values (your-api-token-here, a localhost password), which are placeholders per the .env.example exception, not real committed secrets — and it does NOT flag a completeness or validation gap, because every var read in src/config.ts (DATABASE_URL, API_TOKEN) is documented in .env.example AND validated at startup by the required() guard that throws on a missing var. It records a healthy result and writes its report to zuvo/audits/, modifying no file — no fabricated secret or missing-var finding.
assertions: The audit records no ENV4 secret-exposure finding — the only env file is .env.example with placeholder values (your-api-token-here, a localhost password), recognized as placeholders (the .env.example exception), not real committed secrets.

The audit records that the env vars read in src/config.ts (DATABASE_URL, API_TOKEN) are documented in .env.example and validated at startup via the required() guard — no ENV1 completeness gap and no ENV3 missing-validation gap.
The audit writes a healthy env-audit report to zuvo/audits/, and it modifies no .env or source file.

fixtures.content:
# .env.example — placeholders only, no real secrets.
DATABASE_URL=postgres://user:password@localhost:5432/app
API_TOKEN=your-api-token-here
